But be warned, it's not just any tea, okay? In this video, I'm going to show you the main teas that can help the thyroid according to studies and clinical practice.  The first tea is ginger tea.  It is a root that is extremely rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols.  This is important because many people with thyroid problems, especially rashimoto's, have a condition of chronic inflammation in their bodies.  Ginger can help improve digestion, boost metabolism, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation.  In addition, it can also help people who experience cold hands and feet, and a slower metabolism .  A simple way to consume ginger is to boil 1 to 2 cm of fresh ginger in water for 5 to 10 minutes.  The second tea is turmeric tea, which is very beneficial for the thyroid.  Turmeric contains curcumin, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory compound.  And this is relevant because many thyroid diseases, especially Hashimoto's thyroiditis, are autoimmune diseases.  In other words, there is an imbalance in the immune system. Curcumin may help modulate this inflammatory response.  Important tip: to improve curcumin absorption, it's ideal to use it with black pepper to activate the curcumin, and also with a source of fat.  Healthy, MCT oil, coconut oil, even olive oil. Another interesting tea is lemon balm tea.  It also helps the nervous system and can contribute to reducing anxiety, improving digestion, and aiding relaxation.  The gut has a very important relationship with the thyroid, because a large part of hormonal conversion occurs in the body with intestinal involvement, and maintaining a balanced digestive system can greatly help patients with thyroid problems.  One important point: no tea alone will solve thyroid problems.  Thyroid function depends on several factors: proper diet, specific nutrients, supplementation, gut health, immune system balance , and lifestyle.  Many people forget that the T3 hormone, the active thyroid hormone, comes from T4, and this conversion depends on nutrients obtained from a proper diet.  That's why functional nutrition plays a fundamental role.  If you have symptoms of an underactive thyroid, don't focus solely on medication or isolated solutions.
